fault_hypothesis: Lines 66-67 incorrectly remove a duplicate last element when both lists share the same last value. This post-processing block should not exist — it corrupts the merged result by popping an element that should be preserved.

constraints.intent: After the pop, the merged list must still have length equal to sum of both inputs — the pop violates this invariant.
After the then-branch (pop), length invariant should hold but is broken when both lists share the last element.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left and right and merged__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1]) or len(merged__1) > len(left) + len(right) - 1

constraints.intent: Before the conditional pop, verify that popping is only valid if there truly is a spurious duplicate — which is never the case for a correct merge.
The returned merged list length must equal the sum of both input lengths.
